items.title: Poilievre holds news conference in Ottawa
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre takes questions from journalists after the end of Parliament's spring sitting.

items.title: Seatbelts, everyone! Elizabeth Banks to star as Ms. Frizzle in live-action Magic School Bus movie
items.summary_auto: OK bus, do your stuff! The Magic School Bus is officially heading on a wild field trip to the big screen. Legendary Entertainment and Scholastic Entertainment have announced they’re joining forces to create a live-action feature film adaptation of the beloved children’s book series and animated TV show.

items.title: Canada can now enter Eurovision, but CBC won't say whether it will
items.summary_auto: CBC/Radio-Canada became a full member of the European Broadcasting Union on Thursday, which means Canada is now eligible to enter the Eurovision Song Contest, but the country’s public broadcaster won’t say whether it will participate in the popular competition.

items.title: Carney vows to defend Canadian unity ahead of Alberta's fall referendum
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada's hard-earned reputation as one of the most "trustworthy, reliable and desirable countries" is on the line in Alberta's upcoming separation referendum, warning Thursday that a vote to leave would prompt years of uncertainty at the worst possible time.

items.title: Canadians to learn fate of 24 Sussex on Friday, Carney says
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says he'll announce on Friday what will happen to 24 Sussex Drive — the heritage building sitting vacant for almost a decade after his predecessors allowed it to deteriorate and become uninhabitable.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein's New York rape charge dropped after accuser says she can't endure 4th trial
items.summary_auto: Harvey Weinstein avoided a fourth trial on a New York rape charge after his accuser said she could not endure testifying again, leading prosecutors to drop the #MeToo-era case on Thursday.

items.title: AI crunches global memory chip supply, so Apple and Microsoft hike their prices
items.summary_auto: Apple hiked iPad and MacBook prices on Thursday, saying it could no longer “shield” customers from soaring memory and storage chip costs driven by the AI industry's rapid and expansive data center buildout.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders open ahead of Nov. 19 launch, with a $110 price tag
items.summary_auto: Pre-orders for one of the most-anticipated video games of all time started at midnight Thursday, and gamers will have to put up a sizable sum of cash before leaping into the virtual mayhem set in a fictionalized Florida.

items.title: Fact check: Scam texts link to fake Freedom Mobile websites
items.summary_auto: Scammers are attempting to steal your financial information through fake texts, phone calls and websites impersonating Freedom Mobile.

items.title: 2 people killed in Israeli drone strike on car in South Lebanon
items.summary_auto: An Israeli drone strike on a car in southern Lebanon killed two people on Thursday, medical and security sources said.

items.title: 'It is emotional': Hockey icon Marie-Philip Poulin to undergo knee surgery in July
items.summary_auto: Hockey icon Marie-Philip Poulin will undergo knee surgery in July, Montreal Victoire general manager Danièle Sauvageau announced on Thursday.

items.title: N.W.T. Fire confirms 3 people on board plane that crashed near Fort Simpson
items.summary_auto: Neither the RCMP nor the fire agency provided any information about the status of the people on board. The crash happened about 50 kilometres from Fort Simpson on Wednesday evening.

items.title: Qatar player suspended 5 games after breaking leg of Canada's Koné
items.summary_auto: Qatar player Assim Madibo was given a five-match ban on Wednesday for the tackle that broke the leg of Canada midfielder Ismaël Koné at the World Cup. Madibo was issued a straight red card for the incident during Canada's 6-0 victory over Qatar in Vancouver last Thursday.

items.title: Canadian singer David Clayton-Thomas, who led Blood, Sweat & Tears to hits and Grammys, dead at 84
items.summary_auto: David Clayton-Thomas, the powerhouse Canadian singer who lifted American band Blood, Sweat & Tears to the heights of pop music success, including Grammy awards and one of the biggest-selling albums of its time, has died.

items.title: Garnet found in Mars meteorite surprises scientists
items.summary_auto: Mineral found in a 4.4-billion-year-old meteorite gives potential insight into the history, development and geological processes of Mars.

items.title: As U.S. tensions persist, buy-local movement remains strong in Nova Scotia
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump’s talk of Canada becoming the 51st state made Halifax’s Dale Darling stop buying American products. And she's far from alone, numbers show.

items.title: Step aboard the only replica of a 16th century Spanish galleon, thanks to an 'extraordinary gift' from Canada
items.summary_auto: The San Juan is almost ready to recreate a centuries-old journey to Canada.

items.title: Mark Carney's plan to bulk-buy unsold Vancouver condos might be a bailout, but it doesn't have to be
items.summary_auto: People need affordable homes, and thousands of empty condos in British Columbia are waiting to be sold. The federal government has an idea to help with both: Prime Minister Mark Carney is partnering with the B.C. government to convert vacant condos into affordable housing units.

items.title: Headed to the ER? Here's why you could now wait more than 48 hours to be admitted
items.summary_auto: A report released Thursday by the Canadian Institute for Health Information found that limited emergency room capacity, difficulties in accessing standard care and the country's aging population are to blame for patients spending more than 48 hours waiting in ERs.

items.title: Why an ex-national chief wants to sue Canada over its secret Indigenous spying program
items.summary_auto: Ovide Mercredi says he intends to sue the federal government over its surveillance of Indigenous leaders. It's the latest response to a CBC News investigation into the RCMP's “Native extremism program" in the 1970s. But as the former Assembly of First Nations leader now learns, the spying didn't stop there.

items.title: 'Mayday to Ottawa': $400M carbon capture facility could be cancelled after changes to Alberta's carbon tax
items.summary_auto: A shovel-ready, $400-million proposed facility to convert landfill waste in Edmonton into electricity that took five years to develop, could now be cancelled after the recent carbon tax agreement between the Alberta and federal governments.

items.title: StubHub World Cup ticket cancellations prompt calls for investigations, legal action
items.summary_auto: StubHub customers who had World Cup tickets cancelled at the last minute are calling on lawyers, politicians and the U.S. Federal Trade Commission to investigate the company.

items.title: Military held on to nearly 70% of sexual offence cases after Ottawa promised transfer to civilian police
items.summary_auto: Military police failed to transfer almost 70 per cent of sexual offence cases over the past five years to civilian police, despite the government committing to doing so as it worked to pass a permanent change into law.

items.title: Vancouver port receives permission to dredge Burrard Inlet, allow big oil tankers to increase load
items.summary_auto: The Vancouver Port Authority says dredging underneath the Second Narrows bridge will start in September, but it could not say how long the work will take.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Death toll, injuries rise as search for survivors continues
Swiss collector eager to sell his collection of Indigenous artifacts back to communities
items.summary_auto: A Swiss collector who has amassed thousands of Indigenous artifacts says he is eager to have his collection repatriated back to the communities it has come from.

items.title: Strong back-to-back earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 164 and injuring hundreds
items.summary_auto: Thousands of Venezuelans were feared dead on Thursday after two powerful earthquakes wreaked havoc in and around the capital Caracas, trapping people beneath the rubble of collapsed buildings and setting off powerful aftershocks.

items.title: Trump's D.C. reflecting pool is the butt of online jokes. What happened?
items.summary_auto: The pool's $14.7-million US renovation and subsequent rapid deterioration has become the butt of internet jokes. Some have dubbed June "ALGAEBTQ Pride Month" or commented that Trump, who promised to "drain the swamp" in Washington, has instead created a swamp.

items.title: Halifax-bound flight started 'swerving violently' after captain became incapacitated
items.summary_auto: The flight was en route from Newark to Halifax on Wednesday when the captain experienced a medical emergency in the cockpit. The co-pilot diverted the plane to Boston where it landed safely.
